provider_name: Regional Municipality of Durham Waste Management

plastic-bottle.description: Rinse and place loose in the recycling bin. Do a separate search for bottle caps to see if they are accepted in your area.

glass-bottle.description: Clean glass bottles should go in recycling. Do a separate search for bottle caps to see if they are accepted in your area.

styrofoam-cup.description: Styrofoam cups are generally not accepted in recycling and should go in the garbage.

paper-cup.description: Paper cups are usually lined with a non-recyclable chemical coating; place in garbage.

styrofoam-takeout-container.description: Takeout containers made of styrofoam should be placed in the garbage.

cardboard-box.description: Flatten and place clean cardboard boxes in recycling. Remove plastic packaging first.

cardboard-takeout-container.description: Clean, dry cardboard takeout containers can usually go in recycling if they are not contaminated with food.

apple-core.description: Food scraps such as apple cores belong in the organics or green bin.

disposable-batteries.description: Disposable batteries should be taken to a household hazardous waste drop-off location rather than placed in regular bins.
